incidents.victim: Government agencies, military, critical infrastructure worldwide (200+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Exploited known vulnerabilities in 18,000+ end-of-life SOHO routers (TP-Link, Mikrotik) to hijack DNS settings and intercept OAuth authentication tokens via adversary-in-the-middle attacks on Microsoft 365.
incidents.impact: Harvested credentials, emails, and authentication tokens from 200+ organizations and 5,000 consumer devices across 120+ countries without deploying malware. FBI conducted court-authorized 'Operation Masquerade' to remediate compromised U.S. routers.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerability CVE-2026-35616 (severity 9.1/10) in Fortinet FortiClient EMS allowing unauthenticated remote code execution via crafted requests.
incidents.impact: CISA added to KEV catalog and ordered federal agencies to patch immediately. Second FortiClient EMS vulnerability in three weeks. Exploitation ramped up over Easter weekend when security teams were at reduced staffing.

incidents.victim: University of Mississippi Medical Center; Passaic County, New Jersey
incidents.vector: Medusa ransomware operators exploited web-facing vulnerabilities including zero-days in SmarterMail and GoAnywhere MFT, achieving data exfiltration and encryption within 24 hours of initial access.
incidents.impact: UMMC, the largest hospital in Mississippi, was forced to divert patients and shut down systems; it fully reopened March 2 with FBI and DHS assistance. Passaic County also claimed as victim. Medusa increasingly targets healthcare and municipal government.

incidents.victim: Southeast Asian government entities (dozens of agencies)
incidents.vector: Exploited vulnerability CVE-2026-3502 in TrueConf video conferencing software's update mechanism to distribute malicious payloads through trusted update channels to connected government endpoints.
incidents.impact: Compromised TrueConf on-premises server operated by governmental IT department distributed malicious updates to dozens of government entities. Attackers deployed ShadowPad malware and Havoc penetration testing tool for persistent access.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized network access identified on March 28; specific intrusion method not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Hasbro took systems offline and activated business continuity plan. Order fulfillment delays expected for several weeks. Company still assessing full scope of impact. Shares fell 3% in premarket trading.

incidents.victim: Amazon Web Services Bahrain data center
incidents.vector: Kinetic military strike on AWS cloud computing facility in Bahrain as part of broader Iranian attacks on Gulf infrastructure during the US-Iran conflict.
incidents.impact: AWS Bahrain region taken completely offline. IRGC threatened further waves of strikes against steel industries and major bridges in Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Jordan. Represents unprecedented kinetic targeting of cloud infrastructure.

incidents.victim: WhatsApp users (approximately 200 individuals, primarily in Italy)
incidents.vector: Italian surveillance company ASIGINT (subsidiary of SIO) created a bogus version of WhatsApp rigged with spyware and tricked targets into installing it through a highly targeted social engineering campaign.
incidents.impact: Approximately 200 users compromised with spyware. Second Italian spyware disruption by Meta in 15 months, following the Paragon spyware exposure in early 2025. Italy and Paragon have since parted ways.

incidents.victim: Axios JavaScript library users (estimated 600,000 downloads of compromised versions)
incidents.vector: Hijacked the npm account of the lead Axios maintainer via social engineering (fake video call with fake company), then published malicious versions (v1.14.1, v0.30.4) containing a hidden dependency that deployed cross-platform remote access trojans.
incidents.impact: RAT malware deployed to Windows, macOS, and Linux systems with potential reach into millions of environments. Approximately 600,000 downloads of compromised versions. Axios has 100 million weekly downloads. Google attributed attack to North Korean group UNC1069.

incidents.victim: Jones Day (major U.S. law firm, 2,400 lawyers)
incidents.vector: Phishing attack by Silent Ransom Group targeting firm personnel, gaining access to a limited number of dated client files.
incidents.impact: Client materials for 10 clients accessed and posted online on March 30. The firm represented President Trump in his 2016 and 2020 campaigns. FBI has warned Silent Ransom Group has been targeting law firms since 2023 due to the sensitive nature of legal data.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of critical vulnerability CVE-2026-3055 (severity 9.3/10) in Citrix NetScaler ADC enabling unauthenticated disclosure of sensitive memory, similar to previous Citrix Bleed attacks.
incidents.impact: CISA ordered immediate federal patching. Researchers compared impact to original Citrix Bleed (2023) which was used by ransomware gangs and nation-state hackers against hospitals and critical infrastructure. The NetScaler Gateway serves as the authentication front door for many organizations.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of critical unauthenticated remote code execution vulnerability CVE-2025-53521 in F5 BIG-IP Access Policy Manager, a widely deployed enterprise networking component.
incidents.impact: Active exploitation confirmed by F5 and NCSC. Organizations advised to isolate affected systems and investigate for compromise. BIG-IP APM is common in large enterprises for traffic management and authentication.

incidents.victim: Aqua Security Trivy, Checkmarx KICS, BerriAI LiteLLM, Telnyx SDK, 48+ npm packages, 16+ organizations
incidents.vector: TeamPCP systematically compromised open-source security tools (Trivy, KICS) and AI gateway (LiteLLM) by exploiting stolen credentials and GitHub tokens, injecting infostealer payloads into CI/CD pipelines and PyPI packages.
incidents.impact: Over 300 GB of data exfiltrated from 500,000+ machines including cloud tokens, SSH keys, and Kubernetes secrets. 48 additional npm packages infected. 16 victim organizations published on leak sites. Attackers deployed CanisterWorm with decentralized C2 and wiper capabilities.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP hackers breached the European Commission's AWS account by exploiting a compromised version of the Trivy security scanner received through normal software update channels, then misused a stolen Amazon API key.
incidents.impact: Approximately 92 GB of compressed data stolen including 52,000 email communication files. Data from 42 internal clients and 29 EU entities potentially exposed. Stolen data appeared on ShinyHunters dark web site on March 28.

incidents.victim: Critical infrastructure organizations in Israel, UAE, US, UK, Europe, Saudi Arabia (300+ organizations)
incidents.vector: Password-spraying campaign targeting Microsoft 365 environments across critical infrastructure sectors. Targeting correlated with cities struck by Iranian missiles, suggesting coordination with kinetic operations for bombing damage assessment.
incidents.impact: Over 300 organizations targeted. Campaign overlaps with infrastructure and targeting patterns of known Iranian state-sponsored groups. Represents deliberate integration of cyber operations with military strikes during the US-Iran conflict.

incidents.vector: Pay2Key ransomware deployed using compromised administrative credentials and living-off-the-land techniques against a U.S. healthcare target.
incidents.impact: Assessed by Insikt Group as state-aligned disruption rather than financially motivated — indicating deliberate targeting of U.S. healthcare during the Iran conflict rather than opportunistic crime.

incidents.vector: Russian actors targeting messaging app accounts (WhatsApp, Signal, Messenger) of high-risk individuals by tricking them into sharing login codes, adding attacker devices to accounts, phishing via malicious QR codes, and joining group chats undetected.
incidents.impact: NCSC and international partners issued joint warning about growing malicious activity targeting individuals with access to sensitive information. Campaign builds on earlier FSB and GRU targeting of government officials' messaging accounts.

incidents.vector: German BKA identified 31-year-old Russian national Daniil Shchukin as 'UNKN,' leader of both the GandCrab and REvil ransomware operations, along with co-conspirator Anatoly Kravchuk.
incidents.impact: Law enforcement milestone: Shchukin linked to 130+ cyberattacks causing €35M+ in damages. GandCrab/REvil collectively extorted over $2 billion. Shchukin believed to reside in Krasnodar, Russia and unlikely to be extradited.

incidents.vector: Maryland man Jonathan Spalletta exploited smart contract vulnerabilities in two separate hacks of Uranium Finance in April 2021, draining liquidity pools through deceptive transaction sequences.
incidents.impact: Over $54 million stolen across two hacks, causing Uranium Finance to shut down. Proceeds laundered via Tornado Cash and spent on rare collectibles including a $500K 'Black Lotus' Magic card. $31 million seized by law enforcement in 2025. Spalletta arrested and charged.
